user.title: S.M.A.R.T. Disk Monitor — Connection
user.description: Enter the connection details for the remote host running smartmontools.

data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of the remote Linux host.
data_description.username: The SSH user that has permission to run smartctl.
data_description.auth_type: Choose how to authenticate: password or SSH private key.

password.title: S.M.A.R.T. Disk Monitor — Password
password.description: Enter the SSH password for **{username}** on **{host}**.

key.title: S.M.A.R.T. Disk Monitor — SSH Key
key.description: Paste the PEM-encoded SSH private key for **{username}** on **{host}**.

data_description.ssh_private_key: The full contents of your private key file (e.g. ~/.ssh/id_rsa).

devices.title: S.M.A.R.T. Disk Monitor — Devices
devices.description: Select the block devices to monitor on **{host}**.

data_description.devices: Choose from the discovered devices, or type a device path manually (e.g. /dev/sda).

error.cannot_connect: Unable to connect to the remote host. Check the hostname, port, and network connectivity.
error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed. Check the username and credentials.
error.no_devices_selected: Please select at least one device to monitor.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. See the Home Assistant logs for details.
